skill.name: ci
skill.description: Analyzes CI/CD failures and guides safe fixes for failed builds, tests, and pipeline checks.
skill.summary: Diagnose CI failures, classify root causes, and guide safe test or implementation fixes.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline backticks, multilingual text, and example snippets. The confirmed residual risk is legitimate Bash guidance for CI and test commands, which can execute repository scripts and templated arguments if used without review.

medium_findings.title: External CI and Test Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally guides agents to run gh and npm commands to inspect CI logs and rerun tests. This is expected for CI support, but it can execute repository scripts or unsafe templated arguments if the operator does not review the command first.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines contain concrete shell commands used for CI analysis and test reruns. The commands are legitimate for this skill, but Bash execution creates a real operational risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings mostly point to Markdown code fences, inline paths, placeholders, and output templates. These are documentation examples, not executable Ruby backtick calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the flagged content is in Markdown examples and templates. No Ruby source or active backtick execution was found in these files.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Flag Is a Relative Import Example
low_findings.description: The filesystem alert points to a test mock example that imports ../api inside a TypeScript snippet. It is not code that reads arbitrary paths or accepts user-controlled filesystem input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact location is a vi.mock relative import in documentation. There is no path join, file read, or traversal sink at that line.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Are Frontmatter False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts point to description frontmatter, not hashing or encryption code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithms being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language descriptions. Review found no cryptographic operation in the skill files.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Flags Reflect Documentation Content
low_findings.description: The entropy alerts appear to be caused by Japanese text, tables, code blocks, and symbols in Markdown documentation. No evidence found of binary payloads, encrypted blobs, or encoded malware.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are readable Markdown with frontmatter and examples. No hidden binary or obfuscated content was visible during manual review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Templated Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Commands such as gh run view {{run_id}} --log and npm test -- {{test-file}} depend on placeholders. Operators should validate values before execution to avoid unsafe shell interpretation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documentation templates rather than executable code, but they include variable placeholders that could become unsafe if copied directly into a shell.

content.value_statement: CI failures often hide the real cause behind long logs and repeated test noise. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code classify failures, propose fixes, and avoid unsafe test shortcuts.
actual_capabilities: Reads CI logs and identifies failed jobs, steps, and error categories.
Classifies build, dependency, environment, lint, and test failures.
Suggests root-cause fixes before changing tests or CI settings.
Guides targeted reruns for failed tests and full regression checks.
Provides report formats for CI analysis and test-fix summaries.
limitations: Requires access to CI logs, repository files, or authenticated CLI output.
May run local test commands that execute project scripts.
Cannot confirm product requirements without user or repository context.
Test or CI configuration changes need explicit review when behavior may change.

use_cases.title: Triage a Broken Pull Request
use_cases.description: Find the first meaningful error in CI logs and explain what likely caused it.

use_cases.title: Repair Failing Test Suites
use_cases.description: Compare test expectations with implementation behavior and propose targeted fixes.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Release Pipelines
use_cases.description: Separate dependency, environment, and code failures before a release branch is merged.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a CI Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this CI failure log. Identify the failed step, the first meaningful error, and the most likely fix.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Pipeline Errors
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging across several failure types.
prompt_templates.prompt: Classify these CI errors as build, dependency, environment, lint, or test failures. Explain the priority order for fixing them.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Failing Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted repair after a failing test is identified.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this failing test, its error output, and the implementation. Propose the smallest correct fix and explain why it is safe.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Safe CI Recovery
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with commands, patches, and verification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CI recovery plan for this branch. Include root-cause analysis, command review, patch strategy, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: A GitHub Actions job fails after dependency installation with a resolver error.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies a dependency resolution failure, points to the lockfile or package version conflict, and recommends a controlled reinstall or version adjustment.

output_examples.input: A unit test fails because the received value differs from the expected value.
output_examples.output: The skill compares the assertion with current behavior, decides whether implementation or test expectations changed, and proposes a minimal fix with rerun steps.

output_examples.input: A pipeline has build, lint, and test failures in the same run.
output_examples.output: The skill prioritizes the build failure first, then dependency or lint issues, and leaves downstream test noise until the earlier failure is resolved.

best_practices: Review every shell command before running it, especially commands with placeholders.
Fix implementation defects before changing tests or CI settings.
Rerun the smallest failing scope first, then run broader regression checks.
anti_patterns: Do not skip tests or remove assertions to make CI pass.
Do not update snapshots without verifying the behavior change is intended.
Do not treat later log errors as root causes before checking the first failure.

faq.question: Does this skill run CI systems directly?
faq.answer: It provides commands and analysis guidance. Actual CI access depends on local tools, authentication, and user approval.

faq.question: Can it work with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for GitHub Actions logs and failed run inspection.

faq.question: Can it work with GitLab CI?
faq.answer: Yes. It can analyze GitLab CI logs when the relevant output is provided.

faq.question: Will it modify tests automatically?
faq.answer: It can suggest or apply fixes when the environment allows editing, but test behavior changes require careful review.

faq.question: What failures can it classify?
faq.answer: It covers build errors, dependency failures, environment problems, lint failures, and test failures.

faq.question: Is it safe for production repositories?
faq.answer: Use it with command review and normal code review. It may execute project test scripts during verification.
